_comment: Interpretive English redrafts of Polish notes, keyed by item id. Faithful to meaning, not literal. Test batch of curated favorites — confirm the voice, then scale. Built into notes_en.js by build_notes_en.py.
walkabout: A masterpiece. An improbable, doomed communion between worlds that should never have met — Roeg distils the surreal essence of Australia and finds an aching, wordless beauty in life at the very edge of the world.
ikiru: Every bit the equal of 12 Angry Men — it brings together cinema's very best instincts, and the result stands first-class in both what it says and how it's made.
la-haine: Deftly narrated and directed, with a razor-sharp cross-section of society; it slackens a little midway, but I walked out of the cinema shaken — naturalism in its purest form.
possession: As if The Shining and The Exorcist were fused and redlined — astonishing. Cosmic performances, execution, makeup, and acting. Wow.
burning: How much can be conveyed through such subtle, immaculate craft from the whole ensemble. The second half didn't fully satisfy me, but it's intensely hypnotic cinema.
12-angry-men: Quite literally — the execution of this format is cinema absolute.
oldboy: So much here lands exceptionally — the direction above all, and the balance it strikes between comedy and drama. But the ending wrecked me like nothing has in a very long time.
apocalypse-now: A steady descent into depth and the grotesque, its heavy atmosphere matched by a production as monumental as its subject. Artful, psychologically charged, resonant on many levels — and a superb adaptation.
